atom.desc: Cognitive layer for package design — judgment the toolchain cannot encode

living-abstract.essence: Design judgment for BentOS packages — taste, naming, decomposition, organism-fit
living-abstract.purpose: Provide the cognitive layer that sits above `bentos-agent pkg` — the toolchain validates structure, this atom provides design wisdom
living-abstract.capacity: Break intent into orthogonal atoms with correct scope and boundaries
Position atoms on correct family, axis, and path in capability space
Name atoms with ontological precision — terms that capture essence
Assess design quality beyond what `pkg check` can measure — organism-fit, vitality, naming aesthetics
living-abstract.principle: Run `bentos-agent pkg check` before cognitive assessment. Fix mechanical errors before judging design.
v0.1 = intent, not implementation. Start small, grow through living
Each atom does one thing well. Test: independent lifecycle?
Atoms load into organisms with faculties. Design for composition, not standalone.

living-concrete.knowledge:
ORTHOGONALITY TEST: "Does X's lifecycle = Y's lifecycle?" If no -> separate. SCOPE TEST: "Can I explain DONE in one sentence?" Too broad -> split. FRICTION-AS-SIGNAL: Difficulty placing an atom may mean wrong decomposition.
When unsure: err toward smaller atoms. Molecules compose; splitting is harder.

Every name must pass ALL five: NECESSARY — not derivable from existing names? DISTINCT — no overlap with existing atoms? CLEAR — unambiguous to someone in capability space? BEAUTIFUL — feels right, sounds right? GENERATIVE — composes well with other names?
The toolchain validates NECESSARY and DISTINCT (collision detection). CLEAR, BEAUTIFUL, GENERATIVE require taste. That's this atom's job.

Family question as arbiter: Enables AGENCY itself? -> Faculty Defines WHO the agent IS? -> Soul User-invocable instrument? -> App Reusable portable expertise? -> Skill
Family first, then axis (skills only). The toolchain validates depth and FQDN format. This atom judges whether the PLACEMENT is right.

The #1 design failure: atoms that duplicate faculty concerns.
| Concern | Owner | Atoms must NOT... | | Memory, wake, sleep | anamnesis | ...specify .mem paths or memory mechanics | | Threading, spawning | embodiment | ...describe spawn protocols | | Self-modification | plasticity | ...include evolution protocols | | Places, spatial topology | embodiment + anamnesis | ...define how places work |
THE TEST: "Would this atom work for ANY agent on ANY project?" If it assumes specific identity, paths, or project structure -> redesign.

Ambiguities the toolchain cannot resolve — human judgment territory:
| Ambiguity | Ruling | | Lang: CRAFT or TOOLS? | BOTH — conventions = craft, toolchain = tools | | Storytelling: CRAFT or DISCIPLINE? | CRAFT if deliverable, DISCIPLINE if method | | Critical thinking: DISCIPLINE or META? | DISCIPLINE if external, META if own thinking | | Tool knowledge vs domain knowledge? | Survives tool replacement = DOMAIN | | Industry verticals: DOMAIN? | NO — verticals are molecules, not atoms |
living-concrete.protocol:
Design + build a new atom. Cognitive phases, then toolchain execution.
1. DECOMPOSE: Intent -> lifecycle test -> scope check. One atom or many? 2. PLACE: Family first (the question test). Axis if Skill. 3. NAME: All five criteria. The name IS the atom's identity. 4. ORGANISM CHECK: Faculty duplication? Project contamination? Agent-specific? 5. BUILD: `bentos-agent pkg new {family/path} --desc "..." --essence "..." --purpose "..." --capacity "name: desc" ...` 6. VERIFY: `bentos-agent pkg check {fqdn}` 7. INSTALL: `bentos-agent pkg install {fqdn}`
Steps 5-7 are toolchain. Steps 1-4 are this atom's territory.

Cognitive assessment layer — what `pkg check` cannot measure.
FIRST: Run `bentos-agent pkg check {fqdn}`. Fix any errors/warnings.
THEN assess what requires judgment:
VITALITY: Is the concrete alive and growing? Protocols for each capacity? Knowledge accumulating? Or is it a skeleton with a stub concrete?
ORGANISM-FIT: Faculty duplication check (the table above). Project contamination check. Agent-specificity check.
NAMING AESTHETICS: Does the name pass BEAUTIFUL and GENERATIVE? Does the FQDN read naturally? Does it compose well with siblings?
DESIGN QUALITY: Right scope? Right decomposition? Right family? Would splitting improve it? Would merging with a neighbor?
REPORT by severity. Suggest concrete fixes.
living-concrete.antipattern: Carrying physics knowledge the toolchain validates. If `pkg check` catches it, this atom doesn't need to know it.
Designing atoms as standalone instead of organism modules.
NEVER edit .claude/skills/. Evolve source, then `pkg install`.
